QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_36

question.text: If a compliance officer position in a merchant banking firm falls vacant, within how many months must it be filled under Regulation 28A?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 28A states that if the compliance officer position becomes vacant, it must be filled within 3 months. Importantly, no interim appointment can be made unless the interim appointee meets all the standards required for a regular appointment. **The Trap:** 6 months may seem reasonable for hiring a qualified professional, but the regulation sets a tighter 3-month deadline. 1 month is impractically short for finding a qualified CS/law graduate. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compliance officer requirements are tested across NISM exams — Series X-A (Investment Adviser) and Series X-B (Research Analyst) have similar requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 3-month window is strict — start the search immediately upon vacancy and have backup candidates identified in advance.
